markets.question: Bab el-Mandeb Strait effectively closed by September 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait less than or equal to 10 for any date between market creation and the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait equal to or below 10, or once data has been published for the listed date and no such value has been published.

If no data has been published for the listed date within 14 calendar days (ET) after that date, this market will resolve based on the data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made before data has been published for the listed date will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions made after data has been published for the listed date will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF PortWatch, specifically the “Arrivals of Ships” data published for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/6b1814d64903461b98144a6cc25eb79c.

events.title: Bab el-Mandeb Strait effectively closed by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait less than or equal to 10 for any date between market creation and the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF PortWatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait equal to or below 10, or once data has been published for the listed date and no such value has been published.

If no data has been published for the listed date within 14 calendar days (ET) after that date, this market will resolve based on the data published up to that point. 

Revisions to previously published data points made before data has been published for the listed date will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions made after data has been published for the listed date will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF PortWatch, specifically the “Arrivals of Ships” data published for the Bab el-Mandeb Strait at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730
, including both the chart and downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Escalating Houthi threats in late April 2026 to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait—citing U.S.-Iran tensions under the Trump administration—have intensified geopolitical risks, sustaining Red Sea disruptions that reroute 90% of affected vessels around Africa, adding 10-14 days to transits and boosting freight costs. The Baltic Dry Index hit 2,991 points on May 6, up over 40% monthly, reflecting strained dry bulk capacity amid elevated war risk insurance premiums exceeding $100,000 per voyage. This chokepoint handles 12% of global oil flows, pressuring crude toward $110 per barrel and inflating European import costs. Polymarket trader consensus implies subdued near-term closure odds, backed by U.S.-led naval patrols, but monitors potential Iranian proxy escalation; key catalysts include UN Yemen talks in late May and OPEC+ supply responses.

markets.question: Londrina EC vs. São Bernardo FC: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Brazil Serie B game between Londrina EC and São Bernardo FC, scheduled for May 10 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Londrina EC and São Bernardo FC combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Londrina EC vs. São Bernardo FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Serie B game, scheduled for May 10 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the nascent 2026 Brasileirão Série B campaign, newly promoted Londrina EC and São Bernardo FC occupy adjacent mid-table positions around 11th-14th after three matches apiece, with modest records like 1-1-1 underscoring their even footing and driving trader consensus to razor-thin implied probabilities near 48-46% for the May 10 clash at Estádio Jacy Scaff. Head-to-head tilts heavily toward stalemates—three draws and one Londrina victory across four prior meetings, including 2025 Série C promotion playoff ties—bolstered by both teams' recent draw-prone form amid defensive solidity. No confirmed injuries or suspensions alter lineups significantly yet, though home advantage and schedule rest could tilt momentum for Londrina in this low-scoring matchup prospect.

markets.question: Timofeeva vs. Lachinova: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Maria Timofeeva and Adelina Lachinova in the Istanbul, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA Challenger results.
markets.groupItemTitle: Istanbul: Maria Timofeeva vs Adelina Lachinova Set 1 O/U 8.5

events.title: Istanbul: Maria Timofeeva vs Adelina Lachinova
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Maria Timofeeva and Adelina Lachinova in the Istanbul, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Maria Timofeeva' if Maria Timofeeva advances against Adelina Lachinova.

This market will resolve to 'Adelina Lachinova' if Adelina Lachinova advances against Maria Timofeeva.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Maria Timofeeva enters as the clear trader consensus favorite against wildcard Adelina Lachinova in the WTA 125 Istanbul Open round of 16 on outdoor clay, driven by her No. 136 ranking versus Lachinova's No. 647, seeding advantage, and superior recent clay form. The sixth seed captured the W75 Chiasso title last week, defeating Lisa Pigato 6-2, 6-3 in the final, and advanced here with a 6-4, 6-3 win over Danka Kovinic in the round of 32. Eighteen-year-old Lachinova notched a strong 7-5, 6-1 upset over Anastasia Gasanova but lacks Timofeeva's experience or head-to-head history; no injuries reported for either ahead of this baseline matchup.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Team WE (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Team WE in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 7 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Team WE.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Weibo Gaming holds a clear edge over bottom-table rival Team WE in their LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch, buoyed by a 2-0 head-to-head win on April 22 and a marginally better 3-8 series record (10-17 games) versus WE's dismal 1-9 (3-19), as both vie for playoffs play-in survival via game differential in the double round-robin format. WBG's recent form falters on a two-series skid—1-2 to Invictus Gaming on May 1 and 0-2 to Top Esports on May 2—highlighting struggles against top sides, while Team WE gained rare momentum yesterday with a 2-1 upset over Invictus Gaming, their first series win amid Hard Fearless Draft constraints and patch 26.7's aggressive meta emphasizing veteran execution from WBG's Xiaohu and Jiejie.

markets.question: Map Handicap: SHIN (-1.5) vs Zoun (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the StarCraft II match between SHIN and Zoun in the Global StarCraft II League Group Stage 2 - Group B, initially scheduled for May 7 at 5:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "SHIN" if SHIN wins 2 or more maps than Zoun in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Zoun". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/starcraft2/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/starcraft2/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the 2026 GSL Season 1 Code S Round of 8 Group Stage 2 Group B opener, Zerg SHIN faces Protoss Zoun in a pivotal BO3 that could shape dual tournament advancement to playoffs alongside herO and ByuN. SHIN carries strong momentum into the May 7 matchup, having upset Terran ByuN 5-4 in a Bo9 at iNu's Battles #15 on May 1 and dominating qualifiers by defeating Maru twice and Classic. Zoun secured RO12 advancement from Group A last week (April 29) with a 2-1 record, sweeping Solar 2-0 twice despite a 0-2 loss to Rogue, showcasing resilient defenses in ZvP scenarios. Historical ZvP head-to-heads favor neither decisively, with recent form highlighting SHIN's aggressive playmaking against Zoun's sturdy midgame transitions.

markets.question: Game 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Dplus KIA and KT Rolster. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 2, or if Game 2 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: KT Rolster clings to second place in LCK 2026 Rounds 1-2 standings at 9-2 despite back-to-back losses to Hanwha Life Esports (April 29) and HANJIN BRION (May 3), plus a qualifier defeat to BNK FEARX (May 4), snapping their prior six-win streak that included a 2-1 victory over Dplus KIA on April 15. Dplus KIA, fifth at 6-5, counters with hotter recent form—four straight series wins post-H2H loss (upsets over T1 on April 17, sweeps of BRO, DN SOOPers, BFX)—tempered by a 1-2 defeat to Gen.G (May 2) and a qualifier win over Nongshim RedForce (May 5). This BO3 rematch pits KT's veteran core (Cuzz, Bdd, Aiming) against DK's momentum (Lucid, ShowMaker), with even historical head-to-head (10-10 games) and macro-focused meta tilting trader sentiment toward a competitive clash.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Weibo Gaming and Team WE each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit (LOW) $580 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Meta Platforms (META) shares closed at $612.88 on May 6, 2026, up 1.3% amid bullish trader consensus following the April 29 Q1 earnings beat, with revenue of $56.31 billion and EPS of $10.44 exceeding estimates on robust ad growth and AI efficiencies. Trailing PE stands at 22.27, with TTM EPS at $27.52 and market cap at $1.56 trillion, trading below the 52-week high of $796.25. Analyst average price targets near $830 signal upside potential from revenue trends and competitive positioning in digital advertising, though elevated AI capex raises margin scrutiny. Key watch: Q2 earnings on July 29 and regulatory updates, with end-May resolution approaching amid tech sector volatility.

markets.question: Will Renofa Yamaguchi FC vs. Tegevajaro Miyazaki end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 9, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Renofa Yamaguchi FC vs. Tegevajaro Miyazaki
events.description: This event is for the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for Saturday, May 9, 2026 between Renofa Yamaguchi FC and Tegevajaro Miyazaki.

eventMetadata.context_description: Renofa Yamaguchi holds a slim edge as trader consensus favorite at home in Ishin Me-Life Stadium for this J2/J3 100 Year Vision League West-B clash, reflecting their strong recent form of four straight wins following a 3-0 first-leg loss to Tegevajaro Miyazaki on April 5. Both sides notched convincing victories on April 29—Renofa 2-0 over FC Ryukyu and Miyazaki 3-0 against Giravanz Kitakyushu—bolstering momentum amid Miyazaki's table-topping position and five-match winning streak with potent away scoring. The tight probabilities underscore a closely contested matchup, with Renofa's home advantage countering Miyazaki's superior head-to-head and group-leading goal difference. No major injuries reported, keeping focus on tactical battle and rest factors.

markets.question: Will Andrew Novak win the 2026 Truist Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a tightly contested Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club, with Jake Knapp (15%), Collin Morikawa (14.4%), and Rory McIlroy (14%) clustered atop implied probabilities in this no-cut Signature Event. Knapp's surge to the lead stems from his breakout 2026 form—five top-8 finishes in eight starts, including strong ball-striking that fits Quail Hollow's demanding tee-to-green test and brutal Green Mile closing stretch. Morikawa's elite strokes gained: approach metrics (top-ranked this season) keep him close despite a late withdrawal, while McIlroy draws support from his recent back-to-back Masters triumph, four prior Truist victories here, and bomber's length advantage on the long layout. Xander Schauffele (9.5%) and Cameron Young (8%) loom with recent momentum, underscoring the field's depth amid absent stars like Scheffler, heightening upset potential in variable weather.

markets.question: Will Jacob deGrom win the 2026 AL Cy Young Award?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 American League Cy Young Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cam Schlittler's blistering start for the Yankees, leading the AL with a 1.52 ERA, 0.87 WHIP across 47.1 innings and 1.9 WAR in eight starts, has propelled him to the top of trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2026 AL Cy Young, buoyed by his elite 3% walk rate and 53 strikeouts. Teammate Max Fried trails closely at 12.7% with a 2.39 ERA, 0.89 WHIP, and 1.8 WAR over a league-high 52.2 innings, showcasing workload reliability. Pablo Lopez sits at 10.4% amid solid Twins form but lacks top leaderboard dominance, while Jose Soriano's 1.74 ERA, 54 strikeouts, and co-leading 2.3 WAR (9.8%) highlight Angels surge; Parker Messick (6.9%) and Gavin Williams (5%) impress with sub-2.50 ERAs and strikeout volume, underscoring a wide-open rotation-heavy race vulnerable to regression, injuries, and second-half surges.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a tightly contested Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club, with Jake Knapp (15%), Collin Morikawa (14.4%), and Rory McIlroy (14%) clustered atop implied probabilities in this no-cut Signature Event. Knapp's surge to the lead stems from his breakout 2026 form—five top-8 finishes in eight starts, including strong ball-striking that fits Quail Hollow's demanding tee-to-green test and brutal Green Mile closing stretch. Morikawa's elite strokes gained: approach metrics (top-ranked this season) keep him close despite a late withdrawal, while McIlroy draws support from his recent back-to-back Masters triumph, four prior Truist victories here, and bomber's length advantage on the long layout. Xander Schauffele (9.5%) and Cameron Young (8%) loom with recent momentum, underscoring the field's depth amid absent stars like Scheffler, heightening upset potential in variable weather.

markets.question: Will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit (LOW) $540 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a closely contested 54.5% implied probability against a US-Cuba military clash in 2026, driven by escalating rhetoric from President Trump—including recent statements hinting at taking control of the island—coupled with Pentagon contingency planning and Cuba's public military readiness signals amid an ongoing US oil blockade since February. Cuban officials have denounced these as direct threats of aggression, while bilateral meetings in Havana as recently as late April pressed for political reforms as a diplomatic off-ramp. Absent troop mobilizations or carrier deployments, traders balance hawkish posturing against de-escalation channels; failed talks, Russian arms deals, or executive orders could tip toward clash, while a sanctions relief agreement would solidify no military encounter.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Infinite entered the United21 Season 47 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinal BO3 against Imperial Academy as the clear trader consensus favorite, backed by their #59 HLTV world ranking, a dominant United21 Season 46 title, and 71% win rate across recent tournaments like Parken Challenger and Esplay Elite, driven by standouts sl3nd (1.27 rating) and kreaz. Imperial Academy, a young academy roster at #193 with 50% recent win rate, impressed by topping Group A undefeated (2-0, +16 round differential over LPH and Sangal ALTERS), showcasing map control on Inferno and Ancient. No recent head-to-head or roster changes; Infinite's form edged sentiment despite Imperial's group momentum, in a double-elimination playoff format resolving on series win.

markets.question: Coritiba FBC vs. SC Internacional: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Brazil Série A game between Coritiba FBC and SC Internacional, scheduled for May 9 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Coritiba FBC and SC Internacional combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Coritiba FBC vs. SC Internacional - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Série A game, scheduled for May 9 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Coritiba hosts mid-table rival Internacional in Brasileirão Betano on May 9 at Estádio Major Antônio Couto Pereira, with the home side holding a stronger position around 7th in standings compared to Internacional's 13th after 10 rounds. Coritiba's recent form shows resilience, including a 1-1 draw at Fluminense on April 4, but key absences loom large: forward Rodrigo Rodrigues (knee surgery, out until early June), winger Keno (knee, late April return possible), and goalkeeper Pedro Morisco (dislocated shoulder, late May). Internacional enters off a morale-boosting 1-0 away win over Corinthians on April 5 and a 1-1 home draw with São Paulo, though Kayky remains sidelined. Head-to-head history heavily favors the visitors (7 wins in last 14 meetings), but Coritiba's home form and table edge fuel trader interest amid recovery uncertainties.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Milan be 24°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Malpensa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Malpensa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/it/milan/LIMC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ensemble forecasts from ECMWF and GFS models, aligned with ARPA Lombardia guidance, place Milan's Linate Airport high temperature on May 9 between 23-25°C, explaining the near-tie among these outcomes at trader-implied odds exceeding 80% combined. Following cooler readings of 16-17°C on May 5-6 amid persistent cloud cover and light northerly winds, a building high-pressure ridge over the western Mediterranean is projected to deliver mostly sunny conditions and enhanced daytime heating. Differentiating factors include model spread on boundary-layer stability and afternoon cumulus clouds—ECMWF leans toward 25°C with stronger subsidence, while GFS favors 23-24°C under slightly cooler mid-level air. ARPA Lombardia's next bulletin and 12Z model runs on May 7-8 will clarify resolution odds.

markets.question: Lajal vs. Sweeny: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Mark Lajal and Dane Sweeny in the Wuxi, originally scheduled for May 7, 2026 at 11:00PM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger results.

events.title: Wuxi: Mark Lajal vs Dane Sweeny
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Mark Lajal and Dane Sweeny in the Wuxi, originally scheduled for May 7, 2026 at 11:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Mark Lajal' if Mark Lajal advances against Dane Sweeny.

This market will resolve to 'Dane Sweeny' if Dane Sweeny advances against Mark Lajal.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Wuxi hard-court quarterfinals, trader consensus reflects a razor-thin edge for Mark Lajal at 50.5% implied probability against third seed Dane Sweeny, underscoring the matchup's competitive balance between closely ranked right-handers (No. 158 vs. No. 135) with no head-to-head history. Both advanced via grueling three-set round-of-16 wins—Lajal grinding out a 3h49m marathon 7-6(8), 6-7(2), 7-6(2) over veteran Mikhail Kukushkin after straight-setting Renta Tokuda, while Sweeny overcame qualifier Andre Ilagan 6-3, 6-7(2), 6-4 following a dominant R32 over wildcard Rigele Te. Endurance tested on outdoor hard, odds could tip on recovery speed, first-serve effectiveness, or late fitness updates amid mild weather.

markets.question: Will Austin Smotherman finish in the Top 20 at the 2026 ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic?
markets.description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player finishes in the top 20 at the 2026 ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic tournament, including ties. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If final results are not announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "No". The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

events.title: PGA Tour: ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic Top 20
events.description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player finishes in the top 20 at the 2026 ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic tournament, including ties. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If final results are not announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "No". eventMetadata.context_description: The ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic fields a 121-player opposite-field roster at the renovated Dunes Golf and Beach Club, a par-71, 7,347-yard links-style layout emphasizing strokes gained: approach and putting amid coastal winds, teeing off Thursday with first-round times starting at 10:50 a.m. ET. Brooks Koepka tops betting markets after four top-20s this season including T12 at the Masters, while Davis Thompson leads power rankings on surging form and a 2024 runner-up here; Kevin Yu boasts the best course history with multiple top-10s. Marco Penge's withdrawal for health reasons yesterday elevated Taylor Montgomery into the field, tightening Top 20 props for mid-tier contenders like Tom Kim and Pierceson Coody amid sponsor exemptions and PGA University amateurs vying for FedExCup points.

markets.question: Will Amanda Anisimova be the 2026 Women’s Wimbledon Winner?
markets.description:
Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Wimbledon (https://www.wimbledon.com/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Elena Rybakina leads trader consensus at 31.5% implied probability for the 2026 Women's Wimbledon title, propelled by her 2022 Grand Slam triumph on grass, massive serve thriving on the low-bouncing surface, and scorching 2026 form highlighted by Australian Open and Porsche Tennis Grand Prix (Stuttgart) victories plus a recent Madrid Open third-round win over Qinwen Zheng. Aryna Sabalenka follows at 23.5% as world No. 1 with explosive power suiting quick points but limited grass peaks (semifinals best), while Iga Świątek's 16.6% reflects ongoing clay dominance yet persistent grass-court vulnerabilities despite ranking improvements. No major injuries plague the top trio entering the June grass swing, where historical surface affinity and recent momentum shape the closely contested field.

markets.question: Will Ricky Castillo win the 2026 Truist Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a tightly contested Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club, with Jake Knapp (15%), Collin Morikawa (14.4%), and Rory McIlroy (14%) clustered atop implied probabilities in this no-cut Signature Event. Knapp's surge to the lead stems from his breakout 2026 form—five top-8 finishes in eight starts, including strong ball-striking that fits Quail Hollow's demanding tee-to-green test and brutal Green Mile closing stretch. Morikawa's elite strokes gained: approach metrics (top-ranked this season) keep him close despite a late withdrawal, while McIlroy draws support from his recent back-to-back Masters triumph, four prior Truist victories here, and bomber's length advantage on the long layout. Xander Schauffele (9.5%) and Cameron Young (8%) loom with recent momentum, underscoring the field's depth amid absent stars like Scheffler, heightening upset potential in variable weather.

markets.question: Will Khamenei post 50-54 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the @khamenei_ir account's post count for May 5–12 reflects high uncertainty following a sharp decline in activity since mid-April, with no posts in the past 19 days despite ongoing US-Iran tensions over the Strait of Hormuz highlighted by Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's April 30 statement asserting control amid ceasefire talks. This irregular pattern—bursts around events like Easter (multiple posts April 5) versus prolonged silences post-Ali Khamenei's March martyrdom—keeps probabilities tightly clustered near 25–27% across mid-to-high ranges, including a slight edge for 45–49. Escalation in regional diplomacy, sanctions, or commemorative dates could spur archival statements and boost higher bins; sustained internal leadership transitions or health concerns for Mojtaba might entrench low output below 10.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Shai Gilgeous-Alexander at 71.5% implied probability for Western Conference Finals MVP, driven by Oklahoma City Thunder's defending champion status, top seed dominance, and 1-0 conference semifinals lead over the Lakers following a 108-90 Game 1 rout where Chet Holmgren posted 24 points and 12 rebounds despite Shai's uncharacteristically sloppy outing. Victor Wembanyama's 20.5% reflects San Antonio Spurs' edge in their tied series versus Minnesota Timberwolves after a 133-95 Game 2 blowout, positioning the young star as Spurs' offensive engine. Lower probabilities for Julius Randle (10.9%, Timberwolves), De'Aaron Fox (10.8%, Kings), and Anthony Edwards (6.4%) hinge on unlikely upsets in ongoing semifinals, underscoring OKC-Spurs as the market's expected matchup amid no major injury reports.

markets.question: Will Anthropic flip BTC by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the public valuation of Anthropic surpasses the market capitalization of Bitcoin (BTC) at any point between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For Bitcoin, CoinGecko's data for BTC will be used, currently available at https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/bitcoin. If CoinGecko stops showing relevant data, data from CoinMarketCap will be used instead.

For Anthropic, any public valuation, or private funding round that values the company will be considered. To qualify, the valuation must be explicitly confirmed by Anthropic or an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. 

The resolution source will CoinGecko and Anthropic's official communications; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Trump reduce the deficit before 2027?
markets.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source will be the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) published by the U.S. Department of the Treasury (fiscaldata.treasury.gov). The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

events.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).

The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 84.5% implied probability for "No," driven by Congressional Budget Office projections showing the fiscal year 2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion (5.8% of GDP), with year-to-date figures already exceeding $1 trillion through February amid persistent revenue shortfalls and spending pressures. President Trump's policy mix—extension of 2017 tax cuts adding $1.4 trillion to decade-long deficits, alongside 42% defense spending hikes to $1.5 trillion in the April FY2027 budget proposal—offsets proposed non-defense discretionary cuts, per CBO analysis. Recent tariff policy shifts could further widen shortfalls by $1.1 trillion over ten years, reinforcing fiscal expansion. Key catalysts include final FY2026 Treasury data by September and debt ceiling negotiations.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Busan be 24°C on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Gimhae Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Gimhae Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/busan/RKPK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Korea Meteorological Administration short-range guidance and global ensembles from GFS and ECMWF project Busan highs of 22–24°C on May 8 under persistent clear skies and light southwest winds, fueling the razor-thin trader consensus with 23°C (22.5% implied probability) narrowly leading 24°C (21.5%) and 22°C (19%). Differentiating factors include subtle model spreads in peak afternoon solar insolation versus coastal sea breeze moderation and minor diurnal cloud development, yielding a 1–2°C uncertainty range typical for spring forecasts two days out. This aligns with early May climatology averaging 21–23°C highs; daily KMA updates and new model runs expected by May 7 could sharpen resolution criteria based on official observations at Gimhae Airport.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.title: IL-09 House Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. eventMetadata.context_description: Illinois' 9th Congressional District, with a Cook Partisan Voting Index of D+19, remains a stronghold for Democrats following longtime Rep. Jan Schakowsky's 2025 retirement announcement, which triggered a crowded primary resolved on March 17, 2026, when Evanston Mayor Daniel Biss secured the nomination over 15 challengers. Trader consensus at 92.5% for the Democratic Party reflects the district's consistent double-digit Democratic margins in recent cycles—Biden carried it by over 30 points in 2020—and Biss's fundraising edge heading into the November 3 general election against Republican nominee John Elleson. While a national Republican wave, major scandal involving Biss, or depressed Democratic turnout could challenge this outlook, historical base rates for such safe seats favor the party hold barring extraordinary developments.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's trader consensus lead at 38.6% implied probability in the F1 Drivers' Championship market stems from his dominant start to 2026, capped by a pole-to-win victory at the Miami Grand Prix on May 3—his third consecutive Grand Prix triumph from pole, extending his points lead to 100 over Mercedes teammate George Russell's consistent 80 points from multiple podiums, including a prior win. Russell's 27.5% pricing reflects his reliability amid Mercedes' early Constructors' edge, while Lando Norris (10.8%) benefits from McLaren momentum like the Miami sprint win, but Max Verstappen's 6.3% trails due to Red Bull's relative struggles in recent races. With many Grands Prix remaining, the market weighs Antonelli's record-breaking youth and pace against potential convergence from rivals like Charles Leclerc and Oscar Piastri.

markets.question: Will Pedro Gallese win 2026 MLS Goalkeeper of the Year?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Goalkeeper of the Year.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: MLS: 2026 Goalkeeper of the Year
events.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Goalkeeper of the Year.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins the MLS 2026 Goalkeeper of the Year race as intensely competitive early in the season, with Inter Miami's Luis Barraza leading at 49.6% implied probability, barely ahead of Chicago Fire's Chris Brady (49.0%) after his third clean sheet and MLS Team of the Matchday nod on Matchday 6 (April 6). LA Galaxy's Novak Mićović (48.6%) and San Jose Earthquakes' Daniel (48.6%, second in shutouts with five through limited starts) keep pace via strong shot-stopping, mirroring Brady's four clean sheets that have propelled Chicago's defensive surge. Charlotte's Kristijan Kahlina (44.3%) leads saves volume, but parity in goals against average and clean sheet rates, alongside uncertain team table positions and playoff paths, sustains the bunching among top candidates.

Charlotte's Kristijan Kahlina (44.3%) leads saves volume, but parity in goals against average and clean sheet rates, alongside uncertain team table positions and playoff paths, sustains the bunching among top candidates.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 35°C or below on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Indian Meteorological Department observations show Lucknow's maximum temperature on May 5 at just 28°C—11.4°C below normal—amid partly cloudy skies and thundery developments, continuing a rare cool spell after early-May rains suppressed heat. Forecasts for the coming days predict highs around 31°C with isolated thunderstorms, gusty winds, and light rain likely over Uttar Pradesh, limiting solar heating and surface warming. This IMD data drives the 88% market-implied probability for 35°C or below on May 8, reflecting trader consensus on persistent cloud cover and convective activity overriding typical pre-monsoon peaks near 40°C. New model runs or clearing skies could shift odds, with updates expected in IMD's daily bulletins.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Juanma Moreno of the Partido Popular commands overwhelming trader consensus at 98% implied probability to remain President of the Junta de Andalucía following the May 17 regional election, driven by consistent polling averages over the past month showing his party securing an absolute majority of over 55 seats in the 109-seat Parliament of Andalusia. Recent surveys from Sigma Dos, Target Point, and CIS highlight Moreno's high approval as the top-valued leader, bolstered by vote gains from a declining Vox and stagnant PSOE under candidate María Jesús Montero, amid a campaign kickoff marked by debates and focus on housing and immigration. While structural incumbency and historical precedent favor continuity from his 2022 absolute majority win, low-probability shifts could arise from late scandals, voter turnout surges, or post-election coalition dynamics if polls tighten unexpectedly.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an overwhelming 78.5% implied probability against a Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, driven by the absence of a public S-1 filing four months after the company's confidential SEC submission in January, amid cooling SaaS valuations and tepid tech IPO demand. Secondary market indications peg Discord's private valuation at $7-10 billion—down from $15 billion in its 2021 funding round—reflecting monetization headwinds from its gaming-centric user base and lagging revenue growth to roughly $600-800 million annually, insufficient for robust profitability. Conditional on listing, traders see a 14.1% chance of sub-$15 billion market cap, aligning with compressed multiples; higher brackets under 2% signal skepticism on growth re-rating without enterprise pivot. Watch for S-1 disclosure as the key near-term catalyst.

markets.question: Will Tarik Skubal strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Tsitsipas vs. Machac: Set 1 Games O/U 9.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Stefanos Tsitsipas and Tomas Machac in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 10. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.
markets.groupItemTitle: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Stefanos Tsitsipas vs Tomas Machac Set 1 O/U 9.5

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Stefanos Tsitsipas vs Tomas Machac
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Stefanos Tsitsipas and Tomas Machac in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Stefanos Tsitsipas' if Stefanos Tsitsipas advances against Tomas Machac.

This market will resolve to 'Tomas Machac' if Tomas Machac advances against Stefanos Tsitsipas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stefanos Tsitsipas, a proven clay-court force with multiple deep runs at the Internazionali BNL d'Italia including a final appearance, meets surging Tomas Machac in the first-round clash on Rome's red dirt. Their head-to-head is tied 1-1 after Machac's four-set upset over Tsitsipas at the 2026 Australian Open, but Tsitsipas holds superior surface history and arrives with positive momentum from surviving his Madrid opener while confirming his back is pain-free. Machac counters with strong recent clay form, highlighted by Monte Carlo wins over Sebastian Baez and Carlos Alcaraz, showcasing aggressive returns and baseline power. No injuries reported; expect a tactical battle favoring endurance in best-of-three sets under mild May conditions.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Strasbourg Alsace face Rayo Vallecano de Madrid in the UEFA Conference League semi-final second leg on May 7 at Stade de la Meinau, following the first leg on April 30 at Estadio de Vallecas, with both clubs nursing key injury concerns amid strong European runs. Strasbourg, eighth in Ligue 1, recently overturned a 2-0 deficit against Mainz to reach the semis but fell 0-1 to Nice in the Coupe de France semi-final on April 22, missing Guela Doué (virus), Valentin Barco (ankle/suspension), Aaron Anselmino (hamstring), and season-ending ACL victim Joaquín Panichelli. Rayo, 11th in La Liga, advanced past AEK Athens on aggregate despite doubts over Randy Nteka (discomfort), Luiz Felipe (thigh), Álvaro García (muscle), and Fran Pérez (back). Home advantage and rest could favor Strasbourg, but squad depth will test trader consensus on aggregate progression.

markets.question: Will 80 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz on any day by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a daily number of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above the listed value for any date between market creation and May 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The number of daily transit calls/arrivals includes container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a daily number of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points, made within this market’s timeframe, will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

U.S. "Project Freedom" escorts briefly boosted May 4 traffic to 10 vessels before Trump's pause due to escalation concerns, while Iran's overture to aid commercial ships faces skepticism. War-risk insurance premiums at 1-5% of hull value and 50% jumps in very large crude carrier spot rates underscore economic deterrence. Catalysts include weekly IMF data, France-U.K. multinational missions, and U.S.-Iran negotiations.

markets.question: Will between 13 and 15 albums be ranked #1 on the Billboard 200 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of albums that are ranked number 1 on any official weekly Billboard 200 albums chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026.

Each specific album may only count once, regardless of the number times it reaches the number 1 spot. Different editions of the same album will be considered to be different specific albums if they contain different tracklists.

Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard 200 album chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard 200 album chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

All officially published Billboard 200 Albums charts will be treated as final. Revisions made after the release of a Billboard 200 album chart will not be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official weekly Billboard 200 albums chart, published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/ and through other official Billboard channels; however, a consensus of credible reporting on albums that reached number 1 on the Billboard 200 within this market's timeframe may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 19-21 (41.9%) and 25+ (41.0%) albums topping the Billboard 200 in 2026, reflecting the year's blistering pace of 14 unique No. 1 debuts in the first 19 chart weeks through May 9, driven by powerhouse releases from Taylor Swift, Zach Bryan, J. Cole, Bad Bunny, Bruno Mars, Harry Styles, BTS's three-week Arirang run—the longest yet—and Noah Kahan's recent Great Divide bow. High streaming units and vinyl sales for genre-spanning acts like country, rap, K-pop, and pop have fueled weekly turnover, outpacing historical norms where recent years saw 20-30 crowns. Key swings hinge on second-half schedules crowded with Drake's Iceman, Kendrick Lamar projects, and others; sustained debuts could push toward 25+, while holdovers like Wallen's I'm the Problem might cap at 19-21. Next charts and Luminate data will clarify momentum.

David Brock Smith leads Oregon's Republican U.S. Senate primary at 57.5% implied probability, bolstered by superior fundraising—$24,000 raised versus Jo Rae Perkins' $5,000 as of late March—and key endorsements from Rep. Alek Skarlatos, the Taxpayers Association of Oregon, and chief petitioners of the No Tax on Measure 120 referendum, all announced in early May. These developments signal establishment preference for Smith as the most viable general election challenger to incumbent Sen. Jeff Merkley.
